areas.area: Repo baseline and branch reconciliation (pre-public hygiene)
areas.plan: Do this before the repo is ever public, because two items are irreversible after first push/release. (1) NAME + IDENTIFIER: 'Curio' collides with Zengobi Curio (shipped macOS software) — run a name-clearance pass now; whatever wins, fix the Tauri identifier from com.curio-reader.app (hyphen + .app suffix both break/warn) to reverse-DNS of an owned domain, e.g. app.curioreader.desktop — it is baked into updater artifacts, signing, and macOS bundle paths and is near-immutable after the first release. Scrub the stray 'Flowfeed' branding in PRD §5.1. (2) HISTORY PURGE: the checked-out branch commits a 33-34MB yt-dlp aarch64-apple-darwin binary at src-tauri/bin/; because the repo is still private, rewrite it out with git filter-repo BEFORE first public push (a gitignore alone leaves it in every clone forever). Gitignore src-tauri/bin/ and src-tauri/gen/ (machine output; gen/schemas/capabilities.json is currently a committed empty projection). (3) LICENSE: no LICENSE file exists on any branch despite Cargo.toml saying MIT — add LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE (dual, see governance area) at root immediately; every commit made without it is a legal ambiguity for future contributors. (4) BRANCH RECONCILIATION: origin/claude/fix-tauri-frontend-dist-Cfqby forked at 58e6834 and is a sibling of HEAD's last two commits (bfedb35, 87189ce) — do NOT fast-forward or merge it; cherry-pick its two ideas into the new baseline deliberately: (a) CI must build the frontend before any cargo/clippy step because tauri::generate_context! validates frontendDist and icons at compile time, (b) real (non-placeholder) icons + trimmed gen/schemas. Then delete both claude/* branches; the production rewrite starts from a clean feat/ branch off main. (5) Delete dead dependencies flagged in the dossier (htmd-until-used, mockall, tokio-test, image, @playwright/test-until-configured, @testing-library/svelte-until-used, @typescript-eslint/types, @tauri-apps/plugin-shell JS pkg) so the lockfiles and cargo-deny start honest.

Platform: GitHub Actions, all third-party actions pinned by commit SHA (Renovate maintains pins with version comments), top-level `permissions: {contents: read}`, `concurrency: {group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true}` on every workflow. PR GATE SUITE (all required checks via branch protection): [job rust-check, ubuntu-22.04] cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -D warnings (frontend built first OR a stub-dist `ci` trick — fold in the Cfqby lesson; cache the built frontend keyed on src/ hash and share via actions/cache so clippy+test+build reuse it); cargo nextest run (faster than cargo test, per-test isolation); MSRV check job building curio-core with the pinned minimum toolchain. [job frontend] npm ci; biome ci .; svelte-check --fail-on-warnings; vitest run --coverage (honest thresholds per Quality area). [job ipc-contract] regenerate tauri-specta bindings and `git diff --exit-code src/lib/bindings.ts` — this single job kills the entire snake_case-vs-camelCase defect class that currently breaks mark-read/star/delete/refresh/export at runtime while CI stays green; it is the highest-value gate in the whole design. [job build, matrix] macos-latest (arm64), macos-15-intel or macos-13 (x64), windows-latest, ubuntu-22.04 (deliberately old: pins WebKitGTK 4.1 — install libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf): run `npm run tauri build` NOT `cargo build --release`, so bundler/icon/sidecar/conf regressions are caught; signing steps conditional on `if: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERTIFICATE != '' }}` so fork PRs pass with unsigned artifacts and secrets are never exposed to forks; upload bundles as artifacts (retention 7d). [job e2e-smoke, ubuntu + windows only] WebdriverIO + tauri-driver 5-scenario smoke (boot, add feed against a local wiremock server, article renders sanitized, keyboard nav, vault export writes a file) — non-required until it proves stable for ~a month, then required; no macOS tauri-driver exists, macOS gets the manual release checklist instead. CACHING: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 (workspace-hash keys, per-job prefix), setup-node with cache:npm. NIGHTLY (schedule: cron): (1) feed-corpus regression — replay the recorded nasty-feed corpus through fetch→parse→sanitize→extract→markdown via wiremock, assert snapshot outputs; plus an allowed-failure live-canary job hitting ~20 curated real feeds to catch web drift, auto-updating a pinned tracking issue on failure; (2) cargo audit + osv-scanner (Rust+npm) + cargo-deny check — failures file/refresh a pinned issue; (3) cargo-fuzz 10 min per target with committed seed corpus, crash artifacts uploaded; (4) criterion benches vs stored baseline via benchmark-action/github-action-benchmark, alert threshold 130%, results published to gh-pages; (5) cargo-machete for unused deps; (6) zizmor static analysis over the workflows themselves. Add CodeQL (rust — GA'd from the 2025 public preview — plus javascript-typescript) on PR + weekly. Keep the existing dependency-review gate (fail-on-severity high, GPL/AGPL denylist for the permissive-licensed app tree).

ORCHESTRATION: use tauri-apps/tauri-action (SHA-pinned) — it already solves matrix bundling, updater-artifact generation, and draft-release upload; custom scripting only for checksums/SBOM/manifest steps. Do NOT auto-publish: tauri-action creates a DRAFT release; a human clicks publish (fixes the current workflow's auto-flip). Release flow: release-please (conventional-commits-driven, matches Alex's squash-merge + Conventional Commits house style) opens a release PR maintaining CHANGELOG.md (keep-a-changelog format) and bumping the version; one version source of truth — package.json, with tauri.conf.json using its `version` pointer and a scripts/set-version.mjs syncing Cargo.toml in the release PR (kills the current three-file drift). Tag merge triggers the release workflow. MATRIX: macOS per-arch DMGs (aarch64 + x86_64 — smaller than universal, and updater targets darwin-aarch64/darwin-x86_64 handle it), Windows NSIS + MSI, Linux AppImage + .deb + .rpm. MACOS SIGNING (Alex's action list): enroll in Apple Developer Program ($99/yr); create a Developer ID Application certificate; export .p12; create an App Store Connect API key for notarytool (more reliable in CI than Apple-ID+app-specific-password). Repo secrets: APPLE_CERTIFICATE (base64 .p12), APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_TEAM_ID, APPLE_API_ISSUER, APPLE_API_KEY, APPLE_API_KEY_PATH — tauri-action consumes these natively and staples the notarization ticket. Hardened runtime is Tauri default; because yt-dlp is NOT bundled (see supply chain) there is no sidecar-signing problem. WINDOWS SIGNING 2026: preferred = Azure Trusted Signing (~$10/mo, cloud HSM, integrates via tauri.conf `bundle.windows.signCommand` + trusted-signing-cli; eligibility for individuals has flip-flopped — verify current onboarding rules); fallback for an individual OSS maintainer = Certum Open Source Code Signing certificate (smartcard/cloud, ~€70/yr) via signCommand; last resort = ship unsigned and document the SmartScreen click-through in install docs until reputation accrues. Never a raw .pfx in CI — 2023+ CA rules require HSM-held keys anyway. LINUX: AppImage is the updater-enabled artifact; .deb/.rpm ship with the updater DISABLED (per-target conf overlay) — package managers own updates there; Flathub submission post-1.0 (its no-runtime-binary-downloads rule is satisfied once yt-dlp is external/opt-in); AUR: publish curio-bin PKGBUILD, bumped by a release job; document WebKitGTK 4.1 runtime deps. HOMEBREW: self-hosted tap (owner/homebrew-tap) with a cask auto-bumped by the release workflow; upstream homebrew/cask only after notarization + traction thresholds. WINGET: vedantmgoyal2009/winget-releaser action once Windows signing exists. AUTO-UPDATER: tauri-plugin-updater with `createUpdaterArtifacts: true`; endpoint `https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest/download/latest.json`; beta channel later via a second manifest fed from prerelease tags (v1.2.0-beta.1). KEY MANAGEMENT (write the ADR now): generate the minisign updater keypair with `tauri signer generate` OFFLINE; private key custody in the maintainer's password manager + a sealed offline backup, never in the repo; CI secrets TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY + TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD used only in the tag-triggered release workflow (not PR workflows — removes the fork-exfiltration surface the brief's design had); ROTATION/COMPROMISE PLAN: the pubkey is baked into shipped binaries, so rotation = ship a transitional release signed by the old key whose binary embeds the new pubkey, then flip — document this in docs/release-process.md so it isn't invented during an incident. EVERY RELEASE ATTACHES: SHA256SUMS + minisign signature of it, CycloneDX SBOM, and GitHub build-provenance attestation (actions/attest-build-provenance). Release from the FIRST alpha so this whole pipeline hardens alongside the product instead of being a 1.0-week scramble.

YT-DLP POSTURE (the poison to drain): never bundle it in the .app (breaks notarization, Flathub, distro norms), never auto-execute an unverified download. v1 ships with zero yt-dlp per the scope cut. When the YouTube enricher returns (v1.x, behind the YouTubeEnricher trait): resolution order = (a) user-configured explicit path, (b) app-managed install in app-data-dir — downloads a version PINNED per Curio release from yt-dlp's GitHub releases and verifies against the SHA256 pinned in Curio's source (not fetched at runtime), (c) PATH lookup only as an explicit opt-in setting, never a silent fallback. Updates to the pin ride Curio releases (Renovate PR bumps the pinned version+hash); no runtime self-update. Fix scripts/bundle-ytdlp.sh accordingly (it currently pins 2024.12.23 with zero verification and tauri.conf never bundles the result anyway). RUST: cargo-deny enforced in PR CI (not report-only) with deny.toml — advisories: vulnerabilities=deny, unmaintained/unsound=warn; licenses: allowlist [MIT, Apache-2.0, Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception, BSD-2-Clause, BSD-3-Clause, ISC, Zlib, Unicode-3.0, MPL-2.0(review)]; bans: wildcard versions=deny, duplicate versions=warn; sources: crates.io only. cargo-audit + osv-scanner nightly AND on PR (osv-scanner covers Cargo.lock + package-lock.json in one tool). NPM: `npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high` as a PR gate (runtime deps are few in a Tauri app; keep it strict), osv-scanner as the broader net. LOCKFILES: Cargo.lock and package-lock.json committed; CI installs with `npm ci` and cargo `--locked`. UPDATES: Renovate (not Dependabot — better grouping/pinning): weekly grouped minor/patch PRs, immediate security PRs, lockfile-maintenance monthly, GitHub Actions digest pinning managed automatically, plus a custom regex manager for the yt-dlp version+SHA pin. SBOM + ATTRIBUTION: cargo-cyclonedx + `npm sbom` (or syft over the repo for one combined document) → CycloneDX JSON attached to every release; cargo-about generates THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES.html shipped in the app's About dialog (readability-port crates are Apache-2.0/MPL — attribution is required, and the fixture corpus must stay synthesized/scrubbed so repo licensing stays clean). ACTIONS SUPPLY CHAIN: everything SHA-pinned, no pull_request_target-with-checkout patterns, least-privilege per-job permissions, zizmor in nightly. MSRV: declare rust-version in Cargo.toml, CI-check it, document the bump policy (minor releases may bump MSRV, stated in CHANGELOG).

areas.area: Quality gates and test strategy (honest tiers)
areas.plan: RUST LINTS: rustfmt default profile, checked in CI. Clippy via [workspace.lints.clippy] in Cargo.toml: correctness/suspicious/perf groups deny; pedantic=warn (triaged, not gating); unwrap_used+expect_used deny in non-test code (allow in #[cfg(test)]) — this makes the brief's 'no unwrap' real instead of aspirational; DROP the numeric cognitive-complexity gate (the lint is nursery — the brief's clippy.toml as written enforces nothing). FRONTEND: biome 2.x + eslint-plugin-svelte for template/a11y linting (biome alone cannot lint Svelte templates — the current setup's silent gap) + svelte-check --fail-on-warnings; TypeScript stays maximally strict (the existing tsconfig is good — keep it). IPC CONTRACT AS A GATE: adopt tauri-specta v2 — it generates typed invoke wrappers (argument names included) and event types from the Rust commands, replacing both the hand-drifted src/lib/types/index.ts (whose CommandError shape is already wrong: Rust serializes externally-tagged {User:{...}}|{Internal:{...}}) and the theater gen-types.sh; committed bindings + CI diff job = the invoke contract can never silently drift again. COVERAGE (honest, not the 80/90% fiction): cargo-llvm-cov gates ONLY the pure core crate (parsers, dedupe, sanitize pipeline, markdown, models) at 85% region coverage — cheap and meaningful there; storage/services/commands report-only through Codecov flags (core/storage/frontend split); frontend gate only on src/lib stores+utils at 70%, components report-only. Delete the unreachable vitest thresholds or make them these real numbers. PROPERTY TESTS (proptest) on the invariant-shaped seams: article dedupe key chain (guid→link→hash(title+published)) totality, date normalization always yields RFC3339 UTC, filename sanitization never escapes the vault root and collision policy is stable, OPML parse∘serialize round-trip with curio:* attrs, FTS query builder never produces a MATCH syntax error for arbitrary user input, truncate_text never panics on any UTF-8 (the current byte-slice panic becomes a permanent regression test). FUZZING (cargo-fuzz, nightly 10min/target + seed corpus in-tree, apply to OSS-Fuzz once public): fuzz_feed_parse (arbitrary bytes → parser must not panic/OOM), fuzz_opml_parse, fuzz_content_pipeline (HTML→ammonia→text-extract→markdown: never panics, output contains no <script/on*= — this doubles as the XSS regression harness), fuzz_url_parsers (YouTube/Reddit). INTEGRATION TESTS: wiremock-based fetch-pipeline suite — conditional GET/304 short-circuit, timeout enforcement, redirect cap, size cap, etag/last_modified PRESERVED on error paths (current code clobbers them), permanent-redirect URL update, 410 dead-feed pause; on-disk SQLite suites under WAL with concurrent reader+writer asserting the UI-read-during-refresh case; migration tests against fixture DBs saved from every released schema version. BENCHMARKS: a curio-fixtures crate with a deterministic seeded generator (1000 feeds / 50k articles / realistic HTML bodies) shared by benches, integration tests, and `make seed-db` for manual QA; criterion benches on parse-1k-item-feed, batch-upsert-10k (must prove the single-tx ON CONFLICT rewrite), FTS query @100k, keyset page fetch; nightly compare via github-action-benchmark (alert 130%), CI runner numbers recorded as the published baseline, '2019 4-core laptop' documented as the human target — this converts the PRD's unfalsifiable metrics table into a harness. E2E REALITY TIERS: (1) real & cheap — vitest component tests with @tauri-apps/api mockIPC (this is where the arg-case bug would have been caught); (2) real & bounded — WebdriverIO + tauri-driver smoke on Linux+Windows CI only, 5 scenarios, promoted to required after stabilizing; (3) macOS — no tauri-driver exists: a versioned manual smoke checklist in the release PR template, per OS. Playwright-drives-Tauri and window.__TAURI_TEST_RESET__ hooks from the brief are unimplementable/backdoor fiction — deleted from the plan; any test-mode hooks live behind a compile-time feature absent from release builds.

THREAT MODEL (docs/security/threat-model.md, one page, STRIDE-lite): primary hostile input is FEED CONTENT — the app's core function is fetching and rendering attacker-controlled HTML/XML/URLs; secondary: malicious OPML import, hostile enclosure/media URLs, compromised update channel, dependency supply chain. The current branch demonstrates the worst case: unsanitized feed HTML + {@html} + csp:null + file-writing IPC commands = stored XSS with an arbitrary-file-write primitive. DEFENSE LAYERS (all of these, not one): (1) sanitize-at-ingest — the existing well-configured ammonia allowlist becomes a mandatory pipeline stage; a CI integration test ingests a <script>/onerror fixture and asserts stored content_html is clean (incident-leaves-machinery: every dossier CRITICAL gets a permanent regression test); (2) strict CSP in tauri.conf: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' asset: http: https: data:; media-src 'self' asset: https:; frame-src https://www.youtube-nocookie.com; connect-src ipc: http://ipc.localhost — no more csp:null, and youtube-nocookie + click-to-load façade replaces the tracking embed; (3) frontend belt-and-braces: a single sanitized-render component wraps every {@html} sink and applies DOMPurify locally, with a lint/grep CI check that {@html} appears nowhere else. CAPABILITIES: create src-tauri/capabilities/main.json (currently ABSENT — the {} projection ships no ACL) with core:default plus only what's used; REMOVE tauri-plugin-shell entirely (registered, ungated, unused from JS); add tauri-plugin-opener scoped to http/https for open-in-browser (fixes the broken window.open pattern); no fs plugin — all file writes go through typed Rust commands. PATH DISCIPLINE: export destinations are not free strings over IPC — the vault root is chosen once via the dialog plugin's folder picker and persisted; every write canonicalizes and asserts starts_with(vault_root), filename templates are sanitized ({title} traversal test), writes are atomic tmp+rename (the vault may be git-watched mid-write); same canonicalize-and-contain treatment for theme_id (current '../../' traversal) and cache paths. OUTBOUND FETCH POLICY: one PolicedClient wrapper mandatory for ALL outbound requests (feeds, favicons, images, enclosures, reddit): deny-resolve to loopback/RFC1918/link-local (SSRF), redirect cap 5 with per-hop policy re-check, size caps (feed 10MB, image 20MB, favicon 1MB) enforced via streaming reads, connect/total timeouts (10s/30s), shared pooled client from managed state, honest User-Agent; billion-laughs/entity-expansion fixture test on the XML path; podcast downloads stream to disk with progress + cancellation, never response.bytes(). SUBPROCESS HYGIENE (when yt-dlp returns): validate the URL is youtube-shaped BEFORE exec, pass it after a literal '--' separator (current code is argument-injectable → --exec RCE), absolute managed-install path only (no bare PATH), clean env, kill_on_drop + timeout, --max-comments and --no-playlist caps; Linux opt-in bubblewrap wrap if present — but argument hygiene is the load-bearing control on all three OSes. DISCLOSURE: SECURITY.md with GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting enabled (zero infra, good UX), 90-day coordinated disclosure, supported-versions = latest minor; security fixes get patch releases within the updater channel. CodeQL (rust + js-ts) and the gating dependency-review complete the picture; cargo-audit/npm-audit graduate from report-only to failing gates per the supply-chain area.

STANCE: zero telemetry, zero analytics, zero phone-home, forever — stated in README and enforced by architecture, not policy: PRIVACY.md enumerates EVERY class of network request the binary can make (feed fetches, image/favicon fetches, youtube-nocookie embeds if clicked, reddit public JSON if enabled, yt-dlp GitHub download if the user opts into the managed install, updater manifest check) — this doubles as the marketing asset for the exact post-Omnivore/post-Pocket audience Curio targets, and the PolicedClient chokepoint makes it auditable (one grep proves the list is complete). UPDATER CONSENT: first-run dialog asks 'check for updates automatically?' (default highlighted yes for GitHub-release builds, remembered forever); deb/rpm/Flathub builds compile the updater out entirely, so package-manager users never see a network call they didn't ask for. CRASH REPORTING: no Sentry, no crash SDK, not even behind a feature flag in v1 (a compiled-out integration still invites drift and FUD). Instead: tauri-plugin-log + tracing to rotating files in the platform log dir; a Rust panic hook that writes a structured panic report (message, backtrace, OS/app version, NO user data) to the log dir; on next launch after a crash, a dialog offers 'open log folder' and 'report on GitHub' — the latter opens a prefilled issue-template URL. A 'Copy diagnostics' button in Settings assembles a redacted bundle (log tail, versions, DB row counts and file sizes — never contents or feed URLs unless the user checks 'include feed list') for manual attachment. This is deliberately human-in-the-loop: the user sees exactly what leaves the machine, because nothing leaves the machine on its own.

LICENSE: dual 'MIT OR Apache-2.0' (LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE, SPDX in both manifests). Rationale: Rust-ecosystem convention (frictionless for the curio-core crate to be embedded anywhere — including Alex's own Knowledge-Plane server-side ingestion, and any future contributor's product), Apache-2.0 adds the patent grant MIT lacks, and permissive licensing is itself positioning against Folo's AGPL-client/proprietary-backend trust problem. Argue AGPL down: Curio is a local-first desktop app — AGPL's network clause protects nothing here while costing packager/contributor friction; if a hosted server variant ever exists it can be a separate AGPL repo, and the REAL lock-in defense is the open vault/event schemas, not copyleft. DCO (not CLA) via the DCO app. Run cargo-deny's license audit continuously (readability ports pull Apache-2.0/MPL — attribution shipped in About). NAME: clear the trademark before launch (Zengobi Curio conflict) and own the domain the bundle identifier derives from. GOVERNANCE.md: honest 'personal-first, community-welcome' — the roadmap is partly driven by the maintainer's private Knowledge-Plane use; define wanted vs out-of-scope contributions (burnout shield); bus-factor mitigation = docs/release-process.md complete enough that anyone with the secrets can cut a release, plus the updater-key custody/rotation ADR. CONTRIBUTOR EXPERIENCE: CONTRIBUTING.md fronted by the Tauri-on-Linux system-deps story (script + table), `make setup` as the one command, a devcontainer AND a nix flake (Tauri's Linux dev-env pain is the #1 contributor filter); issue templates with content: bug (asks for the diagnostics bundle), feed-compatibility report (feed URL + expected/actual — this template FEEDS the nasty-corpus, turning users into fixture suppliers), feature request; PR template with the conventional-commit title rule and the manual-smoke checklist for release PRs; CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant); curated good-first-issue label from day one. CONVENTIONS (mirror Alex's house style, which is also just good practice): Conventional Commits enforced by commit-msg hook (lefthook — keep it, delete the brief's .husky ambivalence) AND PR-title check; squash-merge only, PR title becomes the semantic main-line commit; release-please derives CHANGELOG.md (keep-a-changelog) from squash subjects. ADRs: keep the habit the brief started — docs/architecture/decisions/NNNN-slug.md (MADR format); require ADRs for the irreversible set: bundle identifier, updater key scheme, DB schema versioning policy, vault-frontmatter schema, event-log schema, license. VERSIONING & PUBLIC API: semver with the public surfaces explicitly enumerated — (1) vault markdown frontmatter schema, (2) event-log JSONL schema, (3) the curio CLI, (4) GReader/Fever client compat — each export schema carries its own schema_version field with an additive-in-minors/breaking-only-in-majors guarantee documented in docs/integrations/; these become public API the moment one external consumer (the Knowledge Plane) exists, so version them from the first release. DB schema is internal with a forward-only migration guarantee + automatic pre-migration backup. DOCS SITE: Astro Starlight on GitHub Pages, deployed from docs/ on main pushes; content: per-platform install (including honest Gatekeeper/SmartScreen guidance until signing reputation exists), keyboard reference, feed management, the vault-export + event-log integration guide (THE doc that recruits the Obsidian/Logseq early-adopter community), migration guides (Pocket/Omnivore/Instapaper/Feedly/Inoreader), and 'export everything back out' documented as the FIRST command. ROADMAP: GitHub milestones + a pinned Discussion; Discussions for Q&A, issues only for confirmed bugs/accepted work; no Matrix/Discord pre-1.0. CADENCE: alpha releases from the first milestone (pipeline hardening), then time-boxed minors every 6-8 weeks post-1.0 with patch releases as needed; brand assets licensed separately (CC-BY-ND or a simple marks policy) so forks can take the code but not the name.

day_one_priorities: Pre-public hygiene reset: final name + trademark check, fix bundle identifier (no hyphen/.app), add LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE, git filter-repo the 33MB yt-dlp binary out of history, gitignore src-tauri/bin/ + src-tauri/gen/, cherry-pick the Cfqby branch's frontend-before-clippy CI fix + real icons, delete both claude/* branches
IPC contract gate: adopt tauri-specta v2, commit generated bindings, add the CI regenerate-and-diff job (kills the snake_case arg bug class before any rewrite code lands)

Tauri security baseline: strict CSP replacing csp:null, create src-tauri/capabilities/main.json, remove tauri-plugin-shell, add scoped tauri-plugin-opener, and land the sanitize-at-ingest regression test (fixture with <script> must store clean)

CI skeleton with teeth: fmt + clippy -D warnings (workspace lints incl. unwrap_used deny) + nextest + biome/svelte-check/vitest + 3-OS `tauri build` verification, SHA-pinned actions, rust-cache, concurrency-cancel, signing conditional on secrets so fork PRs pass

Supply-chain floor: deny.toml + cargo-deny in PR CI, osv-scanner + cargo-audit nightly, Renovate config (grouped updates, action digest pinning), lockfiles committed and installed with npm ci / --locked

Start the long-lead credentials NOW (they gate nothing else but have weeks of latency): Apple Developer Program enrollment + Developer ID cert + App Store Connect API key; Azure Trusted Signing eligibility check (fallback: Certum OSS cert); generate the tauri updater minisign keypair offline and write the key-custody/rotation ADR

Release pipeline from first alpha: release-please + tauri-action draft-release workflow with single-source version sync, SHA256SUMS + minisign + SBOM + provenance attestation attached, human-gated publish

Test substrate before feature code: curio-fixtures seeded dataset generator (1000 feeds/50k articles), wiremock fetch-pipeline harness, nasty-feed corpus skeleton, cargo-fuzz targets scaffolded (feed/OPML/content-pipeline/URL parsers)

Governance/docs skeletons: SECURITY.md (GitHub private vulnerability reporting enabled), PRIVACY.md (enumerated network-call classes), CONTRIBUTING.md with Linux dev-env story + devcontainer/nix flake, GOVERNANCE.md, issue/PR templates including the feed-compat-report template
